channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge will weigh holding Giuliani in contempt of court after jury's $148 million defamation award
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge is set to hear arguments Friday on whether to hold Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continuing to spread lies about two Georgia election workers after they secured a $148 million defamation judgment against him. Plaintiffs’ attorneys have asked U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to deliver a capstone address on his foreign policy legacy as he makes way for Trump's return
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden will deliver a capstone address to highlight some of his administration's most significant foreign policy achievements.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court denies bid to block public release of special counsel's report on Trump Jan. 6 probe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Thursday denied a bid to block the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral brings together 5 current and former US presidents to honor one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration succeeded Thursday in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea in a deal that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court puts plea deal on pause for 9/11 mastermind KSM: 23 years later, justice for terrorists is delayed again
item.description: A federal appeals court has delayed Friday's scheduled military court hearing where suspected 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two co-conspirators were expected to plead guilty.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Laken Riley Act overcomes filibuster in Senate as Dems give GOP helping hand
item.description: The GOP-led Laken Riley Act defeated the legislative filibuster on Thursday, effectively ensuring it will pass and become law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some notable quotes from Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Jimmy Carter was remembered at his funeral Thursday at Washington National Cathedral as a man of “character” and “something of a miracle” with “prophetic” vision.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US orange juice and steel will be on Canada's list for retaliation if Trump imposes tariffs
item.description: A senior official says Canada is looking at putting retaliatory tariffs on American orange juice, sinks, toilets and some steel products if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump goes ahead with his threat to impose 25% on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After his only child was killed on 9/11, Ken Fairben looked for justice in a far-off military courtroom on the Guantánamo Bay naval base in Cuba. He traveled there multiple times to observe hearings for accused Sept.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis asks Georgia’s top court to review decision booting her from Trump case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis (D) has asked Georgia’s top court to review her disqualification from the election subversion case against President-elect Trump and several allies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio Supreme Court hears arguments in watchdog group's records dispute with state attorney general
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The Ohio Supreme Court heard oral arguments Wednesday in a long-running public records case pitting the state’s top law enforcement officer against a national watchdog group that is digging into his ties with the Republican Attorneys General Association.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Unfinished Democratic priorities dim along with the party’s hold on power in St. Paul
item.description: A range of DFL proposals moving forward at the Capitol last year are likely frozen in place as Democrats have lost full control of the Legislature when they start back up next week.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘Intention’ is to increase debt limit in massive Trump agenda bill
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said congressional Republicans plan to increase the nation’s borrowing limit as a part of a massive party-line bill they are crafting that will encapsulate President-elect Trump’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Judge grants media requests to record Minnesota senator’s burglary trial
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell is set to stand trial later this month on a burglary charge. A Becker County judge on Tuesday ruled that the proceedings can be recorded by media organizations with some limitations.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of DOJ report on Trump case
item.description: The Trump administration has argued the release of the report should be left to the incoming White House.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sales of Minnesota blackout license plates exceed 250,000 in first year
item.description: More than a quarter-million vehicles in the state now have the plates featuring a simple design of white letters and numbers on a black background.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Krueger, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
